caption.full:
Tia Mowry and Cory Hardrict set new co-parenting rules for their kids

Tia Mowry and Cory Hardrict have filed a new custody document outlining ground rules for their post-divorce family life. The former couple agreed to only visit each other's homes with a personal invitation and to wait six months before introducing new partners to their children, Cairo and Cree.

Do you think these new co-parenting boundaries are fair and in the kids’ best interest?

AI-assisted recap by The Screen Report.

youtube.title: Tia Mowry: 6-Month Dating Ban for New Partners
youtube.description:
Tia Mowry and Cory Hardrict have filed new co-parenting rules for their children, Cairo and Cree. The former couple agreed that new partners cannot be introduced until they have been dating for six months. Visits to each other's homes will also require a personal invitation.

AI-assisted recap by The Screen Report.
